subtitle: When Ashoka Sent Piety Riding on the Roads of an Empire

panels.shot: majestic low-angle wide of the emperor on a balcony above the Ganga plain
panels.action: Ashoka stands robed in white and ochre on the high terrace of Pataliputra, hands resting on the stone parapet, gazing over the distant ribbon of the Ganga and the faint lines of roads vanishing into haze.

dialogue.text: In the long years after conquest, Ashoka turned from the sword to dhamma, and sent officers to carry care, restraint and reverence along every road of his vast Mauryan realm.

i18n.hi: लंबे विजयों के बाद अशोक ने तलवार छोड़ी, धम्म अपनाया, और अफसर भेजे जो हर राह पर देखभाल, संयम और श्रद्धा फैलाएं।

panels.shot: broad bird's-eye of the subcontinent with routes marked in dust and stone
panels.action: An expansive landscape stretches from the Himalaya to the southern plateau, with caravan tracks, stone causeways, and river crossings threading through forests, fields and rocky passes, dotted with tiny processions and forts.

dialogue.text: From Pataliputra on the Ganga, Mauryan roads ran like veins through forests, hills and plains, binding Yonas, Kambhojas, Gandharas, Kalingas and peoples of the south into a single, watchful empire.

i18n.hi: गंगा किनारे पाटलिपुत्र से मौर्य सड़कें नसों-सी जंगल, पहाड़, मैदान चीरती, यवन, कंबोज, गांधार, कलिंग और दक्षिण के लोगों को एक चौकस साम्राज्य में बांधती थीं।

panels.shot: dim interior medium shot in the royal library, lamplight on inscriptions
panels.action: Inside a quiet hall lined with palm-leaf manuscripts and polished pillars, Ashoka sits on a low seat, a stylus in hand, studying a palm-leaf text while a clay tablet etched with proto-Brahmi characters rests nearby.

dialogue.text: The king read counsel on rule and order, on tax and spies and punishment, yet his mind turned to another question: how to bind so many hearts without chains of fear alone.

i18n.hi: राजा राज, कर, जासूस और दंड पर ग्रंथ पढ़ता, पर मन में सवाल था: इतने दिलों को कैसे जोड़े, सिर्फ डर की जंजीरों से नहीं।

panels.shot: close-up on Ashoka’s face in half-shadow, eyes intent
panels.action: Ashoka’s face fills the frame, one side lit by a flickering oil lamp, the other in shadow, his brow furrowed, lips pressed, the faint outline of a stupa-shaped pendant at his neck.

dialogue.speaker: ashoka
dialogue.text: Law can quiet a province. What quiets a man’s cruelty to his own?

i18n.hi: कानून सूबे को शांत रखे, पर आदमी की अपने ही पर क्रूरता क्या शांत करे?

panels.shot: medium shot in council chamber, advisors faint in background
panels.action: In a pillared hall with carved railings, Ashoka sits slightly forward on his throne, court officials in varied garments standing respectfully at a distance, while he traces lines on a wax-covered board with a thoughtful hand.

dialogue.text: He knew the sanghas and shrines, the Brahmanas and Ajivikas, the wanderers and householders. His dhamma would not be one sect’s law, but an imperial ethic of restraint and care.

i18n.hi: वह संघों और विहारों, ब्राह्मणों और आजीविकों, संन्यासियों और गृहस्थों सबको जानता था। उसका धम्म किसी एक पंथ का नियम नहीं, साम्राज्य का संयम और करुणा था।

panels.shot: over-the-shoulder shot of Ashoka addressing scribes
panels.action: Ashoka leans forward from his throne toward a row of royal scribes seated on the floor, each holding palm leaves and styluses, listening as he speaks with one hand raised in measured emphasis.

dialogue.speaker: ashoka
dialogue.text: Write this: obedience to parents, kindness to servants, truthfulness, and restraint from slaughter.

i18n.hi: लिखो: माता-पिता की सेवा, नौकरों से नेकी, सत्य बोलना और हिंसा से रोक।

panels.shot: montage-style wide of artisans carving a rock edict in rugged terrain
panels.action: On a rocky hillside, artisans squat against a sheer boulder, chisels poised, as a supervisor points to freshly incised Brahmi characters climbing in tidy lines up the smoothed stone face.

dialogue.text: His words went out in stone. On lonely rocks and polished pillars, edicts spoke of wells and shade trees, of medical herbs for men and beasts, of mercy in the handling of prisoners.

i18n.hi: उसके शब्द पत्थर में उकेरे गए। सुनसान चट्टानों और चमकते स्तंभों पर लेख कुओं, छायादार वृक्षों, मनुष्यों-पशुओं की औषधियों और क़ैदियों पर दया की बातें कहते रहे।

panels.shot: wide shot of laborers planting saplings along a road
panels.action: Along a wide packed-earth road, workers dig shallow pits while others guide young saplings upright, a few already casting delicate shade over a passing ox-cart and a weary traveler drinking at a newly dug roadside well.

dialogue.text: On the great highways, trees were planted for rest and relief. Wells were sunk, and shelters raised, so that the king’s care might be felt in cool water and a patch of shade.

i18n.hi: महामार्गों पर विश्राम को पेड़ लगाए गए। कुएं खुदवाए, सराय बने, ताकि ठंडा पानी और थोड़ी छांव में भी राजा की चिंता महसूस हो।

panels.shot: intimate interior medium shot of Ashoka with a small group of officials
panels.action: In a smaller chamber open to a courtyard, Ashoka sits without diadem on a simple seat, facing a handful of senior officials kneeling with tablets, his posture earnest rather than regal.

dialogue.text: Yet stones and trees could not question or comfort. For this, Ashoka created a new arm of rule: officers of dhamma, mahamatras charged not with tax or troop, but with conscience.

i18n.hi: पर पत्थर और पेड़ न पूछ सकें, न दिलासा दें। इसके लिए अशोक ने राज की नई शाखा बनाई: धम्म के अधिकारी, महामात्र, जिनका काम कर या फौज नहीं, अंतरात्मा था।

panels.shot: medium shot of Ashoka giving explicit orders, map laid out
panels.action: A low table between them holds a rough map scratched on a board; Ashoka’s finger traces routes while he addresses a pair of attentive officials marked by distinctive sashes.

dialogue.speaker: ashoka
dialogue.text: You shall visit towns and villages, women and elders, ascetics and householders, and learn if justice and compassion live there.

i18n.hi: तुम नगर और गांव, स्त्रियों और बुजुर्गों, संन्यासियों और गृहस्थों से मिलो, और जानो कि वहां न्याय और करुणा बसती है या नहीं।

panels.shot: profile close-up of Ashoka emphasizing respect for all sects
panels.action: In profile, Ashoka raises his hand in a calming gesture, his expression firm yet gentle, as the silhouette of a Brahmana, a Buddhist monk, and a householder appear faintly behind him.

dialogue.speaker: ashoka
dialogue.text: See that no sect is reviled. Let each honor the other’s path, and grow by self-control, not by boasting.

i18n.hi: देखो कोई पंथ गाली न खाए। हर एक दूसरे के मार्ग का मान रखे, और बढ़े संयम से, शेखी से नहीं।

panels.shot: dynamic wide of dhamma-mahamatras setting out from Pataliputra gate
panels.action: Through the massive timbered gate of Pataliputra’s palisade, a caravan of dhamma-mahamatras rides and walks, some on horses, some in ox-carts, scrolls and satchels at their sides, the city’s wooden ramparts looming behind.

dialogue.text: The dhamma-mahamatras rode out from Pataliputra: past the city’s stockades, down the riverways, into forests and foreign quarters, bearing no spears, only the king’s questions and his promises.

i18n.hi: धम्म-महामात्र पाटलिपुत्र से निकले: नगर के किलों के पार, नदी-राहों से, जंगलों और विदेशी मुहल्लों में, हाथ में भाले नहीं, सिर्फ राजा के सवाल और उसके वादे।

panels.shot: wide shot of dhamma officers in a provincial town square in the northwest
panels.action: In a bustling northwestern town where Greek-style columns stand beside Indian shrines, a dhamma officer in Mauryan attire speaks with a group that includes a turbaned trader and a man in a Greek himation, while curious children watch.

dialogue.text: In Yavana towns near the northwestern frontier, they walked among Greek and Iranian merchants, explaining the king’s dhamma as piety and humane conduct, words others rendered as eusebeia in their own tongue.

i18n.hi: उत्तर-पश्चिम सीमा के यवन नगरों में वे यूनानी और ईरानी व्यापारियों के बीच चले, राजा के धम्म को श्रद्धा और मानवीय आचरण कहकर समझाया, जिसे वे अपनी ज़बान में ‘यूसेबिया’ कहते थे।

panels.shot: medium shot of dhamma officer listening to a woman in a village courtyard
panels.action: In a village courtyard shaded by a banyan, a dhamma officer sits on a low stool, head bowed attentively as a veiled woman speaks, a child at her side and a worried elder hovering nearby.

dialogue.text: They asked women and elders of their burdens. They noted where husbands beat with impunity, where daughters were denied, where servants were crushed under unpaid labor, and carried these wounds back to the court.

i18n.hi: वे स्त्रियों और बुजुर्गों से उनके बोझ पूछते। जहां पति सज़ा बिना पीटते, जहां बेटियां रोकी जातीं, जहां नौकर बिना मज़दूरी कुचले जाते, उन घावों की खबर दरबार तक लाते।

panels.shot: interior wide of a prison hall with officer inspecting
panels.action: Inside a dim prison hall with heavy wooden doors and barred openings, a dhamma officer walks slowly, counting on his fingers as gaolers stand stiffly, while prisoners watch from within their cells.

dialogue.text: In the prisons they checked chains and confinement. Under Ashoka’s orders, some sentences were lightened, and delays in execution granted, so that family pleas and remorse might be heard.

i18n.hi: जेलों में वे बेड़ियां और कैदखाने देखते। अशोक के हुक्म से कुछ सजाएं हल्की हुईं, फांसी में मोहलत मिली, ताकि घर वालों की पुकार और पश्चाताप सुना जा सके।

panels.shot: wide of royal road with officers counseling a quarrel between neighbors
panels.action: Beside a busy roadside near a cluster of huts, two angry men stand apart, while a dhamma officer between them speaks calmly, open palms outward, a small crowd watching with wary curiosity.

dialogue.text: On the highways they calmed quarrels that could swell into feuds. Dhamma, Ashoka said, meant not only cow-shelters and hospitals, but patience in speech and forbearance in anger.

i18n.hi: मार्गों पर वे झगड़े थामते जो खून-खराबे बन सकते थे। अशोक ने कहा, धम्म का मतलब सिर्फ गोशालें और औषधालय नहीं, बल्कि वाणी में धीरज और क्रोध में संयम भी है।

panels.shot: medium shot of officer speaking to a group of ascetics from different sects
panels.action: Under a grove of trees, a dhamma officer addresses a small gathering of ascetics—some in bark-cloth, some in white, some shaven with robes—who sit cross-legged, listening with composed faces.

dialogue.text: They met with ascetics of many orders, repeating the king’s decree: to honor one’s own sect by practice, and others by kindness, for reviling another path wounds one’s own.

i18n.hi: वे तरह-तरह के संन्यासियों से मिले, राजा का हुक्म दोहराते: अपने पंथ को आचरण से सम्मान दो, दूसरों को नेकी से, क्योंकि दूसरी राह को कोसना अपनी ही राह को चोट देता है।

panels.shot: medium shot in audience hall where officers report back to Ashoka
panels.action: In the audience hall, Ashoka listens as a group of returning dhamma-mahamatras kneel and present scrolls; his face is grave, one hand resting on the carved arm of his seat, the other lightly touching a report.

dialogue.text: Their reports were not of tribute but of conduct. From them Ashoka learned where harsh officials misruled, where his own commands were ignored, where his dhamma was mocked as softness or welcomed as shelter.

i18n.hi: उनकी रिपोर्टें लगान की नहीं, चालचलन की थीं। उनसे अशोक ने जाना कहां कठोर अफसर जुल्म कर रहे, कहां उसके हुक्म अनसुने, कहां उसका धम्म नरमी कहकर ठुकराया या आसरा मानकर अपनाया जा रहा है।

panels.shot: close-up of Ashoka’s hand revising an edict draft
panels.action: On a low writing board, Ashoka’s hand carefully scratches new lines onto a draft edict, crossing out a phrase and inserting another, as an attendant scribe watches, ready to copy.

dialogue.text: He adjusted his own words in later edicts, clarifying that dhamma meant moral works and inner restraint, not mere ritual. He spoke of conquest by dhamma, not by sword, even as his armies remained.

i18n.hi: उसने बाद के लेखों में अपने शब्द बदले, साफ किया कि धम्म का अर्थ नैतिक काम और भीतर का संयम है, सिर्फ रस्म नहीं। उसने कहा, जीत धम्म से हो, तलवार से नहीं, जबकि उसकी सेनाएं बनी रहीं।

panels.shot: wider contemplative shot of Ashoka at a stupa on the outskirts
panels.action: At the edge of the city, Ashoka stands quietly near a modest stupa and a sacred tree, hands joined, while a small group of monks and laypeople stand at a respectful distance.

dialogue.text: As a lay follower of the Buddha he supported monasteries and relics, yet his dhamma edicts rarely spoke the Buddha’s name. His piety and his politics intertwined, each deepening and restraining the other.

i18n.hi: बुद्ध का गृहस्थ अनुयायी होकर उसने विहारों और धातुओं का समर्थन किया, पर धम्म-लेखों में विरले ही बुद्ध का नाम आया। उसका धर्म और उसका राज गहरे घुल गए, एक-दूजे को रोकते और गहराते हुए।

panels.shot: timeless wide of a weathered rock edict in a quiet landscape
panels.action: Centuries later, a weathered rock bearing faint Brahmi letters stands in a lonely clearing, goats grazing nearby and a distant village visible, the stone silent yet enduring in the slanting light.

dialogue.text: His dhamma officers vanished with his line, yet the stones remain, telling of a king who tried to rule an unruly world through wells and words, through law softened by mercy, and power checked by care.

i18n.hi: उसके धम्म अधिकारी वंश के साथ खो गए, पर पत्थर बचे हैं, बताते हुए उस राजा को जो कुओं और शब्दों से राज चलाना चाहता था—कानून को दया से कोमल, और सत्ता को परवाह से बांधकर।

title_i18n.hi: अशोक ने धम्म अधिकारी नियुक्त किए

subtitle_i18n.en: When Ashoka Sent Piety Riding on the Roads of an Empire
subtitle_i18n.hi: जब अशोक ने साम्राज्य की राहों पर धर्म को घोड़े पर चढ़ा भेजा
